world.title: France to Join Steering Committee
world.description: France is joining the steering committee part of a special tribunal investigating Russia’s crime of aggression against Ukraine.

world.title: Australia news live: Ed Husic condemns ‘obscenely sweet’ gas deals; Anthony Albanese says national cabinet to discuss ‘long tail’ of fuel crisis
Canadian Armed Forces provides insight into Portage exercise
world.description: Canadian Armed Forces personnel are conducting a week-long training exercise in Portage la Prairie this week, bringing about 50 Army Reserve soldiers to the city as part of a Civil-Military Cooperation operation designed to simulate support roles in a conflict environment. The exercise, known as BALTIC COOPERATION, runs from April 19 to 25 and involves soldiers working through scenarios that mirror coordination efforts between military forces and civilian populations during large-scale operations. Exercise overview Lieutenant Colonel Jon Baker explains that the training brings together Army Reserve members from across the country to simulate real-world operational conditions. googletag.cmd.push(function() { if($(document).width()<900) { s = googletag.defineSlot('/50748803/pol-all-bigbox', [300, 250], 'div-gpt-1647631135332-bbm1'); s.setTargeting('URL', [window.location.pathname]); s.setTargeting('position', [1647631135332]) s.setTargeting('story_id', 'canadian-armed-forces-provides-insight-into-portage-exercise'); s.addService(googletag.pubads()); googletag.pubads().refresh([s], {changeCorrelator: false}); } });

world.title: Massive Atlantic sargassum blooms traced to west Africa
world.description: Massive blooms of Sargassum seaweed that have inundated coastlines across the Atlantic since 2011 likely originate off the coast of West Africa—forming years before they are visible and overturning long-standing assumptions about where these events begin.
